reference_document_metadata.title: EAFIX Module-Centric Uploaded Files — AI Reference Catalog
reference_document_metadata.purpose: Describe the uploaded module-centric project files in enough structured detail that an AI can decide how to use them without opening the original files for ordinary orientation tasks.

recommended_ai_lookup_order.need: Understand module purpose, responsibilities, process binding, dependencies, and MVP status
Know physical files assigned to modules
Know contract input/output boundaries and required artifact globs
Understand governance lifecycle and validation flow
Understand module group/layer layout and numeric ID direction

detected_conflicts_or_drift.issue: Two incompatible module catalog schemas exist.
detected_conflicts_or_drift.details: module_catalog.schema.json expects numeric module_id values and canonical_symbol; module_catalog.schema (1).json validates the uploaded symbolic-ID module_catalog.json.
detected_conflicts_or_drift.recommended_resolution: Treat module_catalog.schema (1).json as authoritative for the current symbolic catalog. Treat module_catalog.schema.json as the future numeric-ID schema until a migration decision resolves the identity model.

detected_conflicts_or_drift.details: module_catalog.json has 28 records, module_registry.json/YAML has 25 records, modules_extracted.json has 26 records, and the end-to-end map says 28 canonical modules plus SHARED_LIBS.
detected_conflicts_or_drift.recommended_resolution: Use module_catalog.json for current detailed module descriptions. Use module_registry.json for contract-boundary automation. Reconcile loop/deprecated/shared-lib records before enforcing count-based gates.

detected_conflicts_or_drift.issue: Symbolic and numeric module identities coexist.
detected_conflicts_or_drift.details: module_catalog.json and module_registry.json use symbolic IDs; the end-to-end map uses numeric IDs beginning with 5.
detected_conflicts_or_drift.recommended_resolution: Create or maintain a symbol-to-numeric-ID reconciliation table before numeric-ID migration.

detected_conflicts_or_drift.details: 192 of 264 parsed file rows have no CanonicalModules assignment.
detected_conflicts_or_drift.recommended_resolution: Apply the module assignment policy instead of guessing owners.

stable_join_keys.note: Currently symbolic in most structured files.
Future or parallel numeric identity model.
Physical Windows path for repository files.
Useful for aligning module order in the end-to-end flow.

files.detailed_description: A compact JSON Schema for a module_catalog document emitted after PH-05. It enforces a numeric module identity pattern of ^5\d{19}$, requires each module to declare module_id, canonical_symbol, module_name, and module_kind, and intentionally permits additional properties. Use it for the future numeric-ID catalog model, not for the current symbolic module_catalog.json.
authoritative_for: minimum required fields for numeric-ID catalog records

structure.title: Module Catalog Schema
structure.description: Schema for the numeric-ID module catalog emitted after PH-05.

ai_usage_guidance.use_when: Validating or generating a PH-05-style numeric-ID catalog where module_id must look like 50000000000000000001 and each module has canonical_symbol.
ai_usage_guidance.do_not_use_when: Validating the uploaded module_catalog.json, because that catalog uses symbolic IDs such as F1_CONFIG_PREFERENCES and does not include canonical_symbol.
ai_usage_guidance.must_remember: This schema is permissive; additionalProperties is true, so it does not fully constrain all allowed catalog fields.

relationships: Alternative to module_catalog.schema (1).json.
Conflicts with the current uploaded module_catalog.json identity style.

files.detailed_description: A full JSON Schema for the current HUEY_P EAFIX module catalog format. It requires schema_version 1.0.0, document_type module_catalog, catalog_id, and modules. It forbids undeclared top-level fields but defines a broad module object with version, status, purpose, responsibilities, dependencies, key_functions, process bindings, interfaces, filesystem hints, quality gates, MVP classification, tags, and notes.

ai_usage_guidance.use_when: Generating, checking, or extending the current symbolic module catalog.
ai_usage_guidance.do_not_use_when: A downstream tool explicitly requires numeric 20-digit module IDs beginning with 5.
ai_usage_guidance.must_remember: This is the schema that validates the uploaded module_catalog.json.

relationships: Validates module_catalog.json.
More complete but less numeric-ID-focused than module_catalog.schema.json.

files.file_role: Current detailed module catalog / business architecture registry
files.detailed_description: The main current module catalog for HUEY_P EAFIX. It lists 28 module records, including active/planned canonical modules, a loop controller, a health aggregator, and two deprecated legacy aliases. Each module entry includes purpose, responsibilities, dependencies, key functions, process bindings, MVP/defer classification, tags, and sometimes scopes/interfaces. It is the best single file for understanding what each module is supposed to do and how it maps to the end-to-end process.
not_authoritative_for: physical file ownership for every repository file

key_records.purpose: Resolve a stable, immutable configuration snapshot from defaults and overrides.
responsibilities: Load default configuration values
Apply user and environment overrides in deterministic order
Schema-validate the resolved configuration
Emit an immutable ResolvedConfig snapshot with a stable hash
Continue using the last known good snapshot on validation failure

process_binding_summary.validation: Schema validate; stable ordering; hash snapshot
process_binding_summary.failure: Reject invalid config; continue using last known good snapshot

key_records.purpose: Emit schedule ticks at configured calendar polling cadence.
responsibilities: Poll the system clock at configured intervals
Emit ScheduleTick events to downstream consumers
Support frozen-time mode for deterministic testing
Emit HealthReport alert and pause polling if scheduler stalls

process_binding_summary.validation: Frozen-time mode supported for tests
process_binding_summary.failure: If scheduler stalls, emit HealthReport alert and pause polling

key_records.purpose: Poll external economic calendar source(s) and emit raw calendar payloads.
responsibilities: Poll configured calendar source HTTP endpoints on ScheduleTick
Validate HTTP 200 response and payload parsability
Verify presence of source_event_id in each payload
Retry with deterministic backoff on failure
Emit IngestError and skip cycle on persistent failure

process_binding_summary.validation: HTTP 200; payload parsable; source_event_id present
process_binding_summary.failure: Retry w/ deterministic backoff; on persistent failure emit IngestError and skip cycle

Enforce idempotency by message_id

Rollback and retry once on write failure; halt pipeline on persistent failure

process_binding_summary.validation: Idempotent by message_id; ordered by created_utc + tie-breaker
process_binding_summary.failure: Rollback and retry once; on persistent failure halt calendar pipeline

mvp_classification.defer_reason: Calendar ingestion pipeline is deferred for MVP

Attach symbol set and suppression metadata to each trigger
Enforce no-duplicate-trigger-per-event+offset policy
Apply TTL rules to expire stale triggers

process_binding_summary.validation: No duplicate trigger for event+offset; TTL rules applied
process_binding_summary.failure: Default to conservative suppression metadata; emit error event

mvp_classification.defer_reason: Calendar ingestion pipeline is deferred for MVP

process_binding_summary.validation: Timestamp freshness; symbol normalization; reject malformed
process_binding_summary.failure: Emit IngestError; skip malformed ticks

responsibilities: Compute configured indicators from Bar stream
Apply deterministic rounding rules per config snapshot
Pin indicator parameters from ResolvedConfig to prevent drift

process_binding_summary.failure: Emit indicator error; do not output partial snapshots unless flagged

responsibilities: Merge IndicatorSnapshot and CalendarTrigger into a single FeatureFrame

responsibilities: Apply strategy logic to FeatureFrame and PositionSummary

ai_usage_guidance.use_when: An AI needs the functional intent of a module, its dependencies, its expected inputs/outputs, or its process-step role.
ai_usage_guidance.lookup_key: module_id, for example D2_CALENDAR_SOURCE_ADAPTER or R1_RISK_EVALUATOR.
ai_usage_guidance.do_not_use_when: A tool requires numeric 20-digit module IDs; use the end-to-end module map or a numeric catalog instead.
ai_usage_guidance.must_remember: This file validates against module_catalog.schema (1).json, not module_catalog.schema.json.

relationships: Validated by module_catalog.schema (1).json.
Overlaps with module_registry.json/YAML but is richer and more descriptive.
Can be joined to file_module_mapping.csv by symbolic module_id.

files.file_role: Narrative end-to-end module map with numeric IDs and grouped architecture
files.detailed_description: A human-readable architecture map organized into module groups. It frames modules as atomic context units with co-located artifacts, single manifests, and numeric identities matching ^5\d{19}$. It groups the system into infrastructure, data ingest, compute/features, signal/intent, risk, order management, MT4 bridge, reentry, UI/gateway, EA-side MQL4 systems, shared libraries, and non-module infrastructure dependencies. It is useful for architectural orientation, layer/group membership, service-home hints, microservice ports, and high-level data flow.

module_records_parsed_from_text.purpose: Resolve + validate config snapshot; immutable once locked
Emit schedule ticks for polling cadence
Aggregate service health, emit HealthReport alerts

ai_usage_guidance.use_when: An AI needs the big-picture process map, module grouping, module layering, numeric IDs, or the EA-side decomposition.
ai_usage_guidance.do_not_use_when: A machine needs strict schema validation or exact file ownership; use schemas/catalog/mapping instead.
ai_usage_guidance.must_remember: This file uses numeric IDs while module_catalog.json uses symbolic IDs; treat them as related but not automatically identical without a reconciliation table.

relationships: Complements module_catalog.json.
Potential source for numeric-ID reconciliation.
Related to module-centric repository best practices.

files.detailed_description: A CSV inventory mapping repository file paths to service name, file role, canonical module ownership, and test/non-test status. It contains 264 usable rows after ignoring the leading blank line. Most files are currently unmapped, but mapped rows connect service files to module IDs such as D1_MARKET_FEED_ADAPTER, D2_CALENDAR_SOURCE_ADAPTER, E3_MATRIX_LOOKUP, and B1_MT4_ADAPTER_TRANSPORT. This is the most direct file for determining which current source/test/config/plugin files belong to which module.

ai_usage_guidance.use_when: An AI needs to know which physical files probably belong to which module before editing, reviewing, or creating module-specific docs.
ai_usage_guidance.do_not_use_when: The task is only to understand the conceptual process; module_catalog.json is better for that.
ai_usage_guidance.must_remember: A blank CanonicalModules cell means the file has not yet been assigned. Do not invent an owner without applying the module assignment policy.

relationships: Can be joined to module_catalog.json and module_registry.json by symbolic module_id.
Should be produced or checked using the module assignment policy.

files.detailed_description: A YAML registry generated from updated_trading_process_aligned.yaml. It lists 25 module records and defines each module as active, system-owned, and associated with implemented process steps. Each record gives input contract types, output contract types, required implementation/test/schema glob patterns, and validation rules checking that output contracts are implemented. It is the YAML version of module_registry.json and is useful when YAML is the preferred format for tooling or human review.

out_types: CalendarEvent (UTC timestamps, standardized impact, dedup keys)

contracts: CalendarEvent (UTC timestamps, standardized impact, dedup keys)

ai_usage_guidance.use_when: An AI needs contract input/output boundaries and required artifact patterns for modules in YAML form.
ai_usage_guidance.do_not_use_when: The AI needs detailed module responsibilities or MVP classification; use module_catalog.json instead.
ai_usage_guidance.must_remember: This file is semantically identical to module_registry.json in this upload set.

relationships: Equivalent to module_registry.json.
Derived from updated_trading_process_aligned.yaml.
Overlaps with module_catalog.json but is less descriptive.

files.detailed_description: A small JSON summary containing only the extracted module count, module ID list, source file, and extraction timestamp. It lists 26 modules extracted from updated_trading_process_aligned.yaml, including the loop sentinel. It is useful as a quick checklist of expected module symbols, not as a full registry or catalog.

ai_usage_guidance.use_when: An AI needs a fast module-name checklist without detailed metadata.
ai_usage_guidance.do_not_use_when: Any decision requires purpose, dependency, input/output contracts, or implementation ownership.
ai_usage_guidance.must_remember: This lists 26 modules, while module_registry.json has 25 and module_catalog.json has 28 because the files include different scopes and legacy/loop records.

relationships: Derived from updated_trading_process_aligned.yaml.
Overlaps with module_registry.json and module_catalog.json.

files.detailed_description: The JSON version of the module registry generated from updated_trading_process_aligned.yaml. It has the same content as 2026012201111006_MODULE_REGISTRY_v1.0.0.yaml: 25 active system-owned modules, each with input/output contract boundaries, required file patterns, and output contract validation rules. It is more suitable than the YAML file for strict machine processing and JSON-only automation.
not_authoritative_for: current detailed module purposes and MVP classifications

out_types: CalendarEvent (UTC timestamps, standardized impact, dedup keys)

contracts: CalendarEvent (UTC timestamps, standardized impact, dedup keys)

ai_usage_guidance.do_not_use_when: A task requires rich module-level documentation or actual file path inventory.
ai_usage_guidance.must_remember: This is equivalent to the YAML registry in this upload set and can be used as the machine-preferred version.

Can be joined to file_module_mapping.csv by module_id.

files.file_role: Governance flow architecture and file-phase map
files.detailed_description: A markdown architecture document describing the module-contract governance system as a 129-file governance flow. It organizes the system into phases from glossary definition through schema definition, manifest authoring, registry SSOT, validators, generator engine, module assignment, completeness checks, and audit trail. It also records the conflict-resolution baseline completed on 2026-01-21 and summarizes the automation loop: file changes trigger validation, registry updates, derived artifact regeneration, completeness verification, and audit logging.

phase_flow.name: Conflict Resolution / Configuration Alignment
phase_flow.purpose: Resolve authoritative configuration values before governance artifacts are generated or validated.

phase_flow.name: Glossary Patch
phase_flow.purpose: Define shared vocabulary so AI and tools use consistent terms.

phase_flow.name: Schema Definition
phase_flow.purpose: Define machine validation contracts.

phase_flow.name: Manifest Authoring
phase_flow.purpose: Create per-module contract instances.

phase_flow.name: Registry SSOT
phase_flow.purpose: Maintain single source of truth for module/file/contract state.

phase_flow.name: Validators
phase_flow.purpose: Enforce policies and block non-compliant changes.

phase_flow.name: Generator Engine
phase_flow.purpose: Generate derived artifacts and automation outputs from declarations.

phase_flow.name: Module Assignment
phase_flow.purpose: Derive file ownership from override, manifest, and path rules.

phase_flow.name: Completeness Check
phase_flow.purpose: Verify required module artifacts exist and are coherent.

phase_flow.name: Audit Trail
phase_flow.purpose: Capture traceability and evidence for registry changes and generated outputs.

ai_usage_guidance.use_when: An AI needs to understand how glossary, schema, manifests, registry, validators, generators, assignment, completeness, and audit are supposed to connect.
ai_usage_guidance.do_not_use_when: The AI needs the content of a specific module or exact file mapping.
ai_usage_guidance.must_remember: This is a governance architecture reference; it describes how the system should be controlled, not the exact current module implementation.

relationships: Explains how module assignment policy, registries, validators, and completeness checks fit together.
Supports module-centric repository transformation docs.

files.detailed_description: A YAML policy document defining how repository files should be assigned to modules. It establishes a deterministic precedence chain: explicit override wins first, then nearest module manifest, then path-rule inference, then default unassigned. It also defines the expected module manifest fields, search behavior, path-rule algorithm, override behavior, validation rules, conflict resolution, registry format/status, usage examples, governance rules, and performance considerations. This is the correct control document to consult before assigning an unmapped file to a module.

structure.description: Module assignment rules with override > manifest > path_rule precedence

precedence_chain.condition: manifest file exists AND module_id field present
precedence_chain.description: Explicit module declaration in manifest file
precedence_chain.behavior: Read module_id from closest ancestor manifest

precedence_chain.description: Path-based module inference from directory structure
precedence_chain.behavior: Match relative_path against regex patterns in order

validation_rules.name: override_requires_value
validation_rules.rule: if module_id_source == 'override', module_id_override MUST be present

validation_rules.name: manifest_file_exists
validation_rules.rule: if module_id_source == 'manifest', manifest file MUST have existed at scan time
validation_rules.enforcement: warn (manifest may have been deleted after scan)

validation_rules.name: module_id_matches_override
validation_rules.rule: if module_id_override is not null, module_id MUST equal module_id_override

conflict_resolution.resolution: All files get same module_id (from manifest or path rule)
Skip to next precedence level, log warning
conflict_resolution.rationale: Don't fail scan due to malformed manifest

path_rule_algorithm.case_sensitivity: Case-sensitive on Unix, case-insensitive on Windows

module_registry_status.use_case: Validate that module_id references exist, enforce module constraints
module_registry_status.format: YAML or JSON file with module definitions
module_registry_status.current_status: Not implemented (validation skipped if registry doesn't exist)

precedence_chain.behavior: Read module_id from closest ancestor manifest

precedence_chain.description: Path-based module inference from directory structure

conflict_resolution.resolution: All files get same module_id (from manifest or path rule)
Skip to next precedence level, log warning

path_rule_algorithm.case_sensitivity: Case-sensitive on Unix, case-insensitive on Windows

ai_usage_guidance.use_when: An AI needs to assign or audit file ownership deterministically, especially for unmapped rows in file_module_mapping.csv.
ai_usage_guidance.do_not_use_when: The AI only needs a module purpose summary; module_catalog.json is better.
ai_usage_guidance.must_remember: Never guess module ownership. Apply override > manifest > path_rule > default in that order.

relationships: Controls how file_module_mapping.csv should be derived or corrected.
Feeds registry governance and completeness checks.

files.file_role: Documentation backlog and migration-support document map
files.detailed_description: A markdown planning/backlog document listing the documentation needed to support a module-centric repository transformation. It calls for ADRs, migration runbooks, rollback procedures, DAG documentation, developer guides, API/contract docs, script references, observability docs, integration guides, troubleshooting guides, conceptual docs, and project-specific reconciliation docs. It also proposes a documentation hierarchy under docs/architecture, docs/migration, docs/dag_system, docs/developer, docs/contracts, docs/automation, docs/observability, docs/integration, docs/troubleshooting, and docs/concepts.

documentation_categories.category: Specific Missing Docs for This Project
Specific Missing Docs for This Project

ai_usage_guidance.use_when: An AI needs to know what documentation should be created before or during module-centric migration.
ai_usage_guidance.do_not_use_when: An AI needs current source-of-truth module data or validation rules.
ai_usage_guidance.must_remember: This is a backlog/roadmap document. Treat it as planning guidance unless a later SSOT confirms a document already exists.

relationships: Complements the governance flow diagram and module-centric architecture direction.
Should be reconciled with actual repo state before generating missing docs.

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Resolve a stable, immutable configuration snapshot from defaults and overrides.
responsibilities: Load default configuration values
Apply user and environment overrides in deterministic order
Schema-validate the resolved configuration
Emit an immutable ResolvedConfig snapshot with a stable hash
Continue using the last known good snapshot on validation failure

process_binding_summary.validation: Schema validate; stable ordering; hash snapshot
process_binding_summary.failure: Reject invalid config; continue using last known good snapshot

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Emit schedule ticks at configured calendar polling cadence.
responsibilities: Poll the system clock at configured intervals
Emit ScheduleTick events to downstream consumers
Support frozen-time mode for deterministic testing
Emit HealthReport alert and pause polling if scheduler stalls

process_binding_summary.validation: Frozen-time mode supported for tests
process_binding_summary.failure: If scheduler stalls, emit HealthReport alert and pause polling

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Poll external economic calendar source(s) and emit raw calendar payloads.
responsibilities: Poll configured calendar source HTTP endpoints on ScheduleTick
Validate HTTP 200 response and payload parsability
Verify presence of source_event_id in each payload
Retry with deterministic backoff on failure
Emit IngestError and skip cycle on persistent failure

process_binding_summary.validation: HTTP 200; payload parsable; source_event_id present
process_binding_summary.failure: Retry w/ deterministic backoff; on persistent failure emit IngestError and skip cycle

mvp_classification.defer_reason: Calendar ingestion pipeline is deferred for MVP

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Normalize raw calendar entries to canonical CalendarEvent format with UTC timestamps and dedup keys.
responsibilities: Convert all timestamps to UTC
Standardize impact values to canonical enum
Apply dedup policy to eliminate duplicate events
Emit NormalizationError for malformed rows and continue with valid events

process_binding_summary.output_contract: CalendarEvent (UTC timestamps, standardized impact, dedup keys)
process_binding_summary.validation: UTC conversion success; impact in enum; dedup policy applied
process_binding_summary.failure: Emit NormalizationError for malformed rows; continue with valid CalendarEvent

mvp_classification.defer_reason: Calendar ingestion pipeline is deferred for MVP

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Persist domain events in an append-only, ordered event stream.
responsibilities: Accept CalendarEvent and other domain events for persistence
Enforce idempotency by message_id
Maintain ordering by created_utc with tie-breaker
Rollback and retry once on write failure; halt pipeline on persistent failure

process_binding_summary.validation: Idempotent by message_id; ordered by created_utc + tie-breaker
process_binding_summary.failure: Rollback and retry once; on persistent failure halt calendar pipeline

mvp_classification.defer_reason: Calendar ingestion pipeline is deferred for MVP

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Build anticipation triggers from calendar events, controlling trade window opening and suppression metadata.
responsibilities: Compute anticipation windows from CalendarEvent + offset config
Attach symbol set and suppression metadata to each trigger
Enforce no-duplicate-trigger-per-event+offset policy
Apply TTL rules to expire stale triggers
Default to conservative suppression metadata on error

dependencies.reason: Reads anticipation window offsets from ResolvedConfig

process_binding_summary.validation: No duplicate trigger for event+offset; TTL rules applied
process_binding_summary.failure: Default to conservative suppression metadata; emit error event

mvp_classification.defer_reason: Calendar ingestion pipeline is deferred for MVP

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Ingest raw market ticks and emit canonical MarketTick events with timestamp validation and symbol normalization.
responsibilities: Connect to the configured market data source
Validate tick timestamp freshness
Normalize symbol identifiers to canonical form
Reject and emit IngestError for malformed ticks

dependencies.reason: Reads market feed source config and symbol normalization rules

process_binding_summary.validation: Timestamp freshness; symbol normalization; reject malformed
process_binding_summary.failure: Emit IngestError; skip malformed ticks

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Aggregate market ticks into OHLCV bars using deterministic boundaries and tie-breaker rules.
responsibilities: Aggregate MarketTick events into time-bounded bars
Apply deterministic bar boundary and tie-breaker rules
Drop out-of-order ticks per configured policy
Emit error event for dropped ticks

dependencies.reason: Reads bar interval and out-of-order policy from ResolvedConfig

process_binding_summary.validation: Deterministic bar boundaries; tie-breaker rules
process_binding_summary.failure: Drop out-of-order ticks per policy; emit error event

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Compute technical indicators from bar data and emit a deterministic IndicatorSnapshot.
responsibilities: Compute configured indicators from Bar stream
Apply deterministic rounding rules per config snapshot
Pin indicator parameters from ResolvedConfig to prevent drift
Refuse to emit partial snapshots unless explicitly flagged
Emit indicator error on computation failure

dependencies.reason: Reads indicator parameter settings from ResolvedConfig

process_binding_summary.validation: Deterministic rounding; indicator params pinned by config snapshot
process_binding_summary.failure: Emit indicator error; do not output partial snapshots unless flagged

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Assemble IndicatorSnapshot and CalendarTrigger inputs into a stable, fully-declared FeatureFrame.
responsibilities: Merge IndicatorSnapshot and CalendarTrigger into a single FeatureFrame
Guarantee exact declared feature set with no missing fields
Enforce stable ordering of features
Emit packaging error and suppress FeatureFrame on failure

dependencies.reason: Reads declared feature set from ResolvedConfig

process_binding_summary.validation: Exact declared feature set; stable ordering
process_binding_summary.failure: Emit packaging error; do not emit FeatureFrame

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Evaluate feature frames and generate a directional Signal or a SignalSuppressed with enumerated reason.
responsibilities: Apply strategy logic to FeatureFrame and PositionSummary
Emit Signal when conditions are met
Emit SignalSuppressed with enumerated reason when suppressed
Contain no sizing or broker-specific logic
Emit SignalSuppressed with deterministic reason code on internal error

process_binding_summary.validation: Suppression reasons enumerated; no sizing/broker specifics
process_binding_summary.failure: On internal error, emit SignalSuppressed with deterministic reason code

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Convert a Signal into a TradeIntent with stable correlation_id, constraints, and rationale.
responsibilities: Map Signal fields to TradeIntent structure
Carry the stable correlation_id forward from Signal
Attach constraints and rationale to the intent
Reject intent and emit error on conversion failure

dependencies.reason: Reads intent construction rules from ResolvedConfig

process_binding_summary.validation: Stable correlation_id from Signal; constraints + rationale attached
process_binding_summary.failure: Reject intent; emit error event

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Evaluate trade intent against risk policy, compute position sizing, and emit APPROVE or REJECT decision.
responsibilities: Evaluate TradeIntent against RiskPolicy and PortfolioState
Compute deterministic position sizing with rounding rules
Emit RiskDecision with APPROVE/REJECT and reason codes
Default to REJECT with SAFETY_FAIL on rule engine failure
Guarantee idempotent evaluation for identical inputs

process_binding_summary.validation: Idempotent for same inputs; deterministic sizing + rounding
process_binding_summary.failure: On rule engine failure default to REJECT with SAFETY_FAIL reason

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Compile an approved RiskDecision into a fully-formed OrderIntent with client_order_id and idempotency_key.
responsibilities: Map RiskDecision fields to OrderIntent structure
Assign client_order_id per policy
Assign idempotency_key per policy
Emit error and halt progression to routing on compilation failure

dependencies.reason: Reads order ID and idempotency key policy from ResolvedConfig

process_binding_summary.validation: client_order_id + idempotency_key assigned per policy
process_binding_summary.failure: If compilation fails, emit error; do not proceed to routing

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Route an OrderIntent to the correct broker and emit RoutedOrderIntent preserving the original intent hash.
responsibilities: Apply BrokerPolicy to select routing destination
Preserve OrderIntent hash through routing
Emit RoutedOrderIntent with routing metadata
Emit error and reject on routing failure

process_binding_summary.validation: Routing deterministic; preserves OrderIntent hash
process_binding_summary.failure: If routing fails, emit error and reject

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Serialize and deliver RoutedOrderIntent to the MT4 EA adapter using atomic write semantics.
responsibilities: Serialize RoutedOrderIntent to BrokerOrderEnvelope
Write envelope using atomic write+rename to prevent partial reads
Attach sequence number and hash to envelope
Support idempotent resend semantics
Retry on transient failure per deterministic schedule
Emit TransportError and stop on persistent failure

process_binding_summary.step_name: Serialize and send to MT4 adapter
process_binding_summary.validation: Atomic write+rename (no partial reads); sequence + hash; idempotent resend
process_binding_summary.failure: Deterministic retry schedule; on persistent failure emit TransportError and stop

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Execute broker orders via the MT4 Expert Advisor; emit BrokerExecEvent and EAHealth. Pure execution — no strategy or risk logic.
responsibilities: Read BrokerOrderEnvelope and execute broker order
Emit BrokerExecEvent for accept, fill, reject, and close events
Emit EAHealth periodically
Contain no strategy or risk logic; pure execution only
Classify and emit error events; apply retries per deterministic policy

dependencies.reason: Reads BrokerOrderEnvelope written by transport layer
Sends trade orders to broker via MT4 API

process_binding_summary.validation: EA contains no strategy/risk logic; only execution
process_binding_summary.failure: Classify and emit error events; retries per deterministic policy

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Normalize raw broker execution events into canonical ExecutionReport and PositionSnapshot.
responsibilities: Parse BrokerExecEvent fields into canonical structures
Apply dedup and ordering policy to incoming events
Apply canonical rounding rules to numeric fields
Emit NormalizationError and quarantine malformed broker events

dependencies.reason: Reads normalization and rounding rules from ResolvedConfig

process_binding_summary.step_name: Normalize broker events to canonical reports
process_binding_summary.validation: Dedup + ordering policy; canonical rounding
process_binding_summary.failure: Emit NormalizationError; quarantine malformed broker events

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Apply execution reports to OMS state, emit order/position state changes, and produce TradeClosedRaw on fill completion.
responsibilities: Maintain in-flight order state keyed by client_order_id
Apply ExecutionReport to advance order state machine
Handle idempotent fills and out-of-order events per policy
Emit OrderStateChanged and PositionStateChanged events
Emit TradeClosedRaw when a position is fully closed
Quarantine stream and halt new orders on OMS inconsistency

dependencies.reason: Uses RoutedOrderIntent to initialize order state

process_binding_summary.step_name: Apply execution reports to OMS state
process_binding_summary.validation: Idempotent fills; out-of-order handling per policy; stable final state
process_binding_summary.failure: On OMS inconsistency, quarantine stream and halt new orders

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Classify a closed trade with canonical close reason and compute deterministic PnL.
responsibilities: Map TradeClosedRaw fields to canonical TradeClosed structure
Assign close reason from enumerated enum set
Compute PnL using deterministic rounding rules
Emit TradeClosed with COMPUTATION_ERROR flag if PnL cannot be computed

dependencies.reason: Receives TradeClosedRaw when a position closes
Reads PnL rounding rules from ResolvedConfig

process_binding_summary.step_name: Classify trade close and compute canonical PnL
process_binding_summary.validation: Close reason enum; deterministic PnL rounding rules
process_binding_summary.failure: If PnL cannot be computed, emit TradeClosed with COMPUTATION_ERROR flag

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Map a TradeClosed event to an enumerated OutcomeBucket for downstream reentry decision logic.
responsibilities: Apply stable bucket mapping rules to TradeClosed
Emit OutcomeBucket using only enumerated bucket values
Emit UNKNOWN bucket and quarantine for review on mapping failure

dependencies.reason: Reads bucket mapping rules from ResolvedConfig

process_binding_summary.validation: Only enumerated buckets; stable bucket mapping
process_binding_summary.failure: If mapping fails, emit UNKNOWN bucket and quarantine for review

responsibilities: Evaluate proximity of current time to calendar events
Assign EventProximity to a configured proximity bucket
Validate thresholds have no overlaps or gaps

Reads proximity bucket thresholds from ResolvedConfig

Apply deterministic fallback hierarchy when primary lookup fails
Pin matrix version from ResolvedConfig

consolidated_module_reference_from_modul.purpose: Build a reentry TradeIntent from MatrixDecision, or emit ReentrySuppressed, enforcing chain depth and circular chain guards.
responsibilities: Convert MatrixDecision and ReentryChainState into a new TradeIntent
Enforce hard cap: no reentry beyond configured max depth (R2)
Prevent circular chains
Emit ReentrySuppressed with deterministic reason on suppression or error

responsibilities: Loop: reentry intent follows same risk->order->route->transport->execute chain

process_binding_summary.step_name: Loop: reentry intent follows same risk->order->route->transport->execute chain
process_binding_summary.output_contract: Either (a) new RoutedOrderIntent executed, producing new OutcomeBucket, or (b) suppression/cooldown state update

module_count_reconciliation.interpretation: These counts are not automatically contradictions; each file has a different scope. They must be reconciled before hard validation gates enforce a single count.
